title: Klaviyo vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Klaviyo and Buttondown email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for your needs.
intro: Klaviyo and Buttondown are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Klaviyo targets e-commerce businesses with advanced automation and integrations, while Buttondown focuses on developers and newsletter writers who prefer markdown. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right tool for your specific needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Markdown writing, API access, paid subscriptions

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need SMS marketing and predictive analytics
You want advanced segmentation and automated customer journeys
You require deep integrations with Shopify, WooCommerce, or similar platforms
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown and want developer-friendly tools
You need an affordable option with paid subscription features for readers
You value API access and want to build custom integrations yourself

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Klaviyo is superior for e-commerce businesses needing advanced automation, SMS, and predictive analytics. Buttondown excels for individual creators and developers who prioritize simplicity, markdown writing, and lower costs. Each platform dominates in its respective market.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Klaviyo or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month versus Klaviyo's $45/month. Both offer free tiers, but Buttondown's free tier covers up to 100 subscribers while Klaviyo allows 500, making Buttondown more budget-friendly overall for small creators.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Klaviyo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import and export functionality. Buttondown explicitly offers import/export features, and Klaviyo's integrations and API capabilities make migration feasible. Plan the transition during a low-activity period to ensure seamless data transfer and minimal disruption.
